task_completion.description: Create the required layers with correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List panel in LibreCAD. Verify the presence of layers: LOT-BOUNDARY (White), STRIPING (Yellow), ADA (Blue), and DIMENSIONS (Green).
Look at the drawing canvas. Verify a white rectangle representing the outer boundary of the parking lot.

Look at the drawing canvas. Verify yellow lines forming the parking layout: a horizontal line separating the drive aisle from the spaces, and multiple vertical divider lines for the individual spaces.
Look at the drawing canvas. Verify blue hatching in the narrow access aisle (second section from the left) and blue text 'ADA' in the first space on the left.

task_completion.description: Add linear dimensions to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the drawing canvas. Verify green dimension lines and numbers showing the measurements: 57, 18, 24, and 9.
Look at the LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog to verify the file is named 'parking_lot.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical interface to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with LibreCAD's drawing tools, layer panels, and menus, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script or text editor to bypass the CAD software.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing Python scripts, bash commands, or text editors generating the DXF file directly.

integrity.description: The drawing is genuinely created within the CAD software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing must appear as editable vector lines within the LibreCAD workspace, not as an image opened in an image viewer or web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file path is /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/parking_lot.dxf. The required layers and their AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) codes are: LOT-BOUNDARY (7/White), STRIPING (2/Yellow), ADA (5/Blue), and DIMENSIONS (3/Green). LibreCAD uses these standard ACI color codes.
